title: Charge terminal design for guided surface waveguide probe
abstract: Disclosed is an exemplary guided surface waveguide probe configured to launch a guided surface wave along a surface of a lossy conducting medium. In one embodiment, the guided surface waveguide probe comprises a charge terminal elevated to a height above the lossy conducting medium; a support structure that supports the charge terminal; at least one section of internal coil that is supported within the support structure and is coupled to an excitation source; a conductive tube conductively coupled to the at least one section of internal coil at a bottom end, and a plurality of coupling conductors that extend radially away from a top of the conductive tube to a plurality of points located on an inner surface of the charge terminal.
claims: Claims Therefore, the following is claimed: 1 . An apparatus comprising: a guided surface waveguide probe (500) configured to launch a guided surface wave along a surface of a lossy conducting medium (503), wherein the guided surface waveguide probe (500) comprises: a charge terminal (520) elevated to a height above the lossy conducting medium (503), the charge terminal (520) comprising a hollow hemisphere portion (680) at a top of the charge terminal (520) that transitions into a hollow toroid portion (681 ) at a bottom of the charge terminal (520), wherein the hollow toroid portion (681 ) turns to an inside of the charge terminal (520) and ends at an annular ring lip (682); a support structure (530) that includes a truss frame support structure (531 ) that supports the charge terminal (520); at least one section of internal coil (651 ) that is supported within the truss frame support structure (531 ) and is coupled to an excitation source (212); a conductive tube (612) conductively coupled to the at least one section of internal coil (651 ) at a bottom end, wherein the conductive tube (612) extends vertically towards the charge terminal (520), wherein the at least one section of the internal coil (651 ) is electrically coupled to the charge terminal (520) via the conductive tube (612); and a plurality of coupling conductors (690) that extend radially away from a top of the conductive tube (612) to a plurality of points located on an inner surface of the charge terminal (520). 2. The apparatus of claim 1 , further comprising a top support platform (670) supported at a top of the truss frame support structure (531 ) that is inside the charge terminal (520). 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the top support platform (670) resides within the charge terminal (520). 4. The apparatus of any one of claims 2 to 3, wherein the plurality of coupling conductors (670) are connected to the conductive tube (612) at a portion of the conductive tube (612) that is located below a base of the top support platform (670). 5. The apparatus of any one of claims 2 to 3, wherein the plurality of coupling conductors (670) are connected to the conductive tube (612) at a portion of the conductive tube (612) that is located above a base of the top support platform (670). 6. The apparatus of any one of claims 2 to 5, wherein the conductive tube (612) rises through an opening in the top support platform (670). 7. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein a mounting ring (720) is attached to vertical support bars (710) of the truss frame support structure (531 ) and is secured to the annular ring lip (682) of the charge terminal (520). 8. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the conductive tube (612) comprises an upper portion (612A) of the conductive tube (612) and a lower portion (612B) of the conductive tube (612), the upper portion (612A) of the conductive tube (612) being characterized by a diameter that is less than a diameter of the lower portion (612B) of the conductive tube (612), wherein the plurality of coupling conductors (690) are connected to the upper portion (612A) of the conductive tube (612). 9. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the plurality of points are located at the hollow hemisphere portion (680) of the charge terminal (520). 10. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the plurality of points are located at the hollow toroid portion (681 ) of the charge terminal (520). 1 1 . The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 10, further comprising a lumped element tank circuit (260), wherein the at least one section of internal coil (651 ) is coupled to a ground through the lumped element tank circuit (260). 12. A method comprising: elevating a charge terminal (520) to a height above a lossy conducting medium (503) via a support structure (530) of a guided surface waveguide probe (500), wherein the charge terminal (520) comprises a hollow hemisphere portion (680) at a top of the charge terminal (520) that transitions into a hollow toroid portion (681 ) at a bottom of the charge terminal (520), wherein the hollow toroid portion (681 ) turns to an inside of the charge terminal (520) and ends at an annular ring lip (682) ; supporting at least one section of internal coil (651 ) within the support structure (530); electrically coupling a conductive tube (612) to the at least one section of internal coil (651 ) at a bottom end, wherein the conductive tube (612) extends vertically towards the charge terminal (520) of the conductive tube (612); and connecting a plurality of coupling conductors (690) formed of conductive material that extend radially away from a plurality of first connection points at the top of the conductive tube (612) to a plurality of second connection points located on an inner surface of the charge terminal (520). 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising launching a guided surface wave along a surface of the lossy conducting medium (503) by way of the guided surface waveguide probe (500). 14. The method of any one of claims 12 to 13, wherein the plurality of second connection points are located at the hollow toroid portion (681 ) of the charge terminal (520). 15. The method of any one of claims 12 to 14, further comprising imposing a charge on the charge terminal (520) to synthesize an electric field based upon a voltage applied to the charge terminal (520) from the excitation source (212).
